2603.27529.title: Cross-attentive Cohesive Subgraph Embedding to Mitigate Oversquashing in GNNs
2603.27529.paper_summary: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have achieved strong performance across various real-world domains. Nevertheless, they suffer from oversquashing, where long-range information is distorted as it is compressed through limited message-passing pathways. This bottleneck limits their ability to capture essential global context and decreases their performance, particularly in dense and heterophilic regions of graphs. To address this issue, we propose a novel graph learning framework that enriches node embeddings via cross-attentive cohesive subgraph representations to mitigate the impact of excessive long-range dependencies. This framework enhances the node representation by emphasizing cohesive structure in long-range information but removing noisy or irrelevant connections. It preserves essential global context without overloading the narrow bottlenecked channels, which further mitigates oversquashing. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that our model achieves consistent improvements in classification accuracy over standard baseline methods.
2603.27529.paper_summary_zh: 圖神經網絡（GNNs）在各種現實世界領域中取得了強勁的表現。<lb/>然而，它們面臨著過度壓縮的問題，長距離信息在通過有限的消息傳遞路徑時被扭曲。<lb/>這一瓶頸限制了它們捕捉重要全局上下文的能力，並降低了它們的性能，特別是在圖的密集和異質區域。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種新穎的圖學習框架，通過交叉注意的凝聚子圖表示來豐富節點嵌入，以減輕過度長距離依賴的影響。<lb/>這個框架通過強調長距離信息中的凝聚結構來增強節點表示，同時去除噪聲或不相關的連接。<lb/>它保留了重要的全局上下文，而不會使狹窄的瓶頸通道過載，進一步減輕了過度壓縮的問題。<lb/>在多個基準數據集上的廣泛實驗表明，我們的模型在分類準確性上相較於標準基準方法取得了一致的改善。
2603.27529.author: Tanvir Hossain et.al.
2603.27529.authors: Tanvir Hossain, Muhammad Ifte Khairul Islam, Lilia Chebbah, Charles Fanning, Esra Akbas
